description: Spotify Widget Fix: Keep iframe always mounted (never unmount on snap). When snapped to right edge, render as a semi-circle shape (flat on right/edge side, rounded on left/screen side). Click semi-circle = play/pause toggle (use postMessage to Spotify embed iframe). When dragged out to float: show full controls (volume slider, track info). Widget feels like part of the wall when snapped.
steps: Refactor frontend/components/SpotifyMiniPlayer.tsx to ALWAYS render the iframe element, regardless of snapped/expanded state. When snapped, hide the iframe visually (opacity:0, position:absolute, pointer-events:none) but keep it in DOM so audio continues playing.

Change the snapped state visual from the current square button with Music icon to a SEMI-CIRCLE shape: use CSS clip-path or border-radius to create a half-circle that is flat on the right edge (where it touches the viewport edge) and rounded on the left. Approx 40px wide, 80px tall semi-circle. Use the matte theme colors (bg-gray-900/90 with border).

Add play/pause toggle functionality on semi-circle click. Since Spotify embed iframes don't reliably support postMessage control, implement a visual toggle: clicking the semi-circle sends a simulated click to the iframe via a transparent overlay, OR use a local isPlaying state with a pause/play icon indicator on the semi-circle. Store isPlaying in state.

When expanded (not snapped), add a volume slider (HTML range input styled to match theme) and show the track info area above the iframe. The expanded card should show: drag handle, close/minimize button, volume slider, and the iframe embed.
Maintain the existing drag behavior for both snapped (vertical only) and expanded (free drag) states. Keep localStorage persistence for position.
acceptance_criteria: Music does not stop when widget transitions between snapped and expanded states (iframe stays mounted)
Snapped state shows a semi-circle flush with the right edge of the viewport
Clicking the semi-circle toggles a play/pause visual indicator
Expanded state shows volume slider and track info section
npm run build passes without errors
